subCriterions.name: Project Structure and Initial Data Load

aspects.description: Initial data loads from assets when no saved browser state exists
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Clear site data/localStorage for the app origin, reload the page. The schedule shows 120 training days from assets/data/training.json and MITS pickers use assets/data/collection.json.

aspects.description: Single-page layout with header, sidebar, work area, and footer
aspects.extraDescription: Test: On load, verify all four regions are visible without navigating to another URL.

aspects.description: Sidebar navigation switches views with active item highlighted
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Click Month (Calendar), Kanban, and Statistics. Each shows the correct view and the selected menu item is visually distinguished.

aspects.description: Code organization and maintainability
judgementScoreDescription: Code is unstructured, duplicated, or difficult to follow; files and responsibilities are unclear
Basic separation of HTML/CSS/JS (or framework modules) exists but naming and structure are inconsistent
Code is organized with clear file/module structure, meaningful names, and logical separation of data, UI, and business rules
Excellent modular structure with consistent patterns, reusable components or modules, and code that is easy to extend and maintain

aspects.description: Import shows a confirmation dialog before overwriting stored state
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Import a valid exported file. A confirmation dialog appears before overwrite; canceling leaves current data unchanged.

aspects.description: Import rejects invalid JSON or rule-breaking data with an error message
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Import malformed JSON or data violating business rules. A clear error is shown and stored data is unchanged.

aspects.description: Invalid changes in the day modal are rejected with a visible error message
aspects.extraDescription: Test: In the day details modal, attempt an invalid action (e.g. 4th event on a day, 0 or fractional hours, or exceeding 9 daily hours). The change is blocked and an error appears without corrupting data.

aspects.description: User-friendly validation and business-rule error messages
judgementScoreDescription: Errors are missing, cryptic, or do not explain why the action failed
Errors appear for some invalid actions but messages are generic or hard to understand
Most invalid actions show clear, human-readable messages describing the rule violated
All tested invalid actions show specific, actionable messages matching project examples (9-hour limit, whole hours 1–9, 122-day cap, third scheduling of the same event type)

aspects.description: Header shows GIFTS logo, title, theme toggle, fullscreen, export, and import
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Verify assets/images/gifts-logo.png is shown with the Training Manager title and header controls for theme, fullscreen, export, and import.

aspects.description: Footer shows HRDKorea copyright and Powered by MITS link to skillsit.eu
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Footer displays an HRDKorea copyright notice and a working Powered by MITS link to https://skillsit.eu.

aspects.description: Light/dark theme can be toggled from the header
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Click the theme toggle. The interface switches visibly between light and dark mode.

aspects.description: Light/dark theme choice persists after page reload
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Switch theme, reload the page. The same theme is applied without toggling again.

aspects.description: GIFTS color palette is applied to UI accents and type indicators
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Task indicators use palette blue, evaluation indicators use palette green, and other indicators use palette purple — recognizable in both light and dark modes.

aspects.description: Calendar day status styling distinguishes past, today, and future days
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Past days appear dimmer, today has a highlighted border, future days use normal appearance — without a text label reading Today.

aspects.description: Fullscreen mode can be toggled from the header
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Fullscreen control enters and exits browser fullscreen (or equivalent full-viewport mode).

aspects.description: Provided SVG icons are used appropriately in the UI
judgementScoreDescription: Icons from assets/svgs/ are not used or are broken
Some provided SVG icons are used but usage is incomplete or inconsistent
Navigation, header actions, and view controls use appropriate icons from assets/svgs/
Icons are used consistently with good sizing, alignment, and visual harmony in both themes

aspects.description: Overall visual design quality and cohesion
judgementScoreDescription: Layout is unstyled or cluttered with poor readability
Functional styling with readable content; layout may differ from design reference images if requirements are met
Visually cohesive design with consistent typography, spacing, and GIFTS brand colors; own design choices are acceptable without pixel-perfect match to references
Polished, professional interface with excellent visual hierarchy; creative layout that fully meets functional requirements

aspects.description: Calendar, Kanban, and Statistics views are well structured and clear
judgementScoreDescription: One or more views are missing, empty, or confusing
All views are present and usable but layout or labeling leaves ambiguity
Each view is clearly laid out with understandable labels and navigation
All views are exceptionally clear with intuitive navigation and consistent patterns

aspects.description: Responsive layout usable on desktop resolution
judgementScoreDescription: Main features are broken or unusable at typical desktop width (~1280px)
Core features work but with noticeable layout issues
Header, sidebar, calendar, kanban, and statistics remain usable at desktop width
Layout adapts well at desktop width with no overlapping elements or truncated controls

aspects.description: Schedule changes persist after page refresh
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Add or edit an event, change kanban status, reload the page. All changes remain.

aspects.description: Export downloads human-readable JSON of the current schedule
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Click Export. A formatted JSON file downloads with version, trainingPeriod, and days/events matching on-screen data.

aspects.description: Exported JSON contains no runtime-only event IDs
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Export after using the app. Event objects contain only type, name, durationHours, kanbanStatus, and description (for other) — no UI-generated id fields.

aspects.description: Import replaces persisted state and refreshes calendar, Kanban, and statistics
aspects.extraDescription: Test: After confirming import of a valid export file, all three views reflect imported data and reload still shows imported state.

aspects.description: Deleting the last event on a day removes that day from persisted state
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Delete the only event on a day, reload. That date no longer has events in the schedule (verify via export or persisted storage).

aspects.description: Each day allows 1–3 events only
aspects.extraDescription: Test: On a day with 3 events, adding a 4th is blocked with an error.

aspects.description: Event duration is whole hours between 1 and 9; daily total does not exceed 9 hours
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Reject 0, fractional, or 10+ hour durations. Block additions that would exceed 9 hours on one day.

aspects.description: A collection entry may be scheduled at most twice as task
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Schedule the same collection.json entry as task on two separate days — allowed. A third task scheduling of that entry is blocked.

aspects.description: A collection entry may be scheduled at most twice as evaluation
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Schedule the same collection.json entry as evaluation on two separate days — allowed. A third evaluation scheduling of that entry is blocked.

aspects.description: At most 122 days may have events; cannot schedule to a new date when already at 122
aspects.extraDescription: Test: When 122 days already have events, adding or rescheduling to a date without events is blocked. Events may still be moved among dates that already have events.

aspects.description: Task and evaluation events are selected only from collection.json
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Task/evaluation pickers only accept names present in collection.json.

aspects.description: Other events require name, description, and whole-hour duration
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Other events without description or with invalid duration are rejected.

aspects.description: Monthly calendar grid is shown with previous and next month navigation
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Open the calendar view. A monthly grid is visible. Use previous/next controls to navigate between months within the training period (2026-02 through 2026-08).

aspects.description: Day cells with events show sequence number, name summary, total hours, and type badges
aspects.extraDescription: Test: On a populated day, cell shows #n, name (or Multiple different events), total hours (e.g. 6h), and applicable [T]/[E]/[O] badges using the GIFTS color palette. Empty days show only the day number.

aspects.description: Calendar shows the current month on startup
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Clear site data, reload the app, open the calendar. The grid displays the current calendar month.

aspects.description: Days outside the training period appear dimmed on the calendar grid
aspects.extraDescription: Test: In months overlapping period boundaries, dates before 2026-02-01 or after 2026-08-31 are visually dimmed.

aspects.description: Day details modal displays full event list with collection displayName for MITS events
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Click a day with events. Modal shows date, sequence #n, and each event's fields including displayName and kanbanStatus for task/evaluation, and description for other.

aspects.description: Events can be added and deleted in the day details modal
aspects.extraDescription: Test: In the modal, add a valid event and delete an event via the trash icon. Changes appear after modal close. No inline calendar editing.

aspects.description: Events can be rescheduled in the modal with date field and Move button
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Change an event date via inline edit or date picker and click Move. Event moves to the target day within the training period.

aspects.description: MITS task picker filters by event type and applies default durations
aspects.extraDescription: Test: When adding task, picker lists only collection entries with fewer than 2 task assignments and shows each name; default duration is estTime. For evaluation, fewer than 2 evaluation assignments; default 2 hours (editable).

aspects.description: Modal closes via Esc, outside click, or Close button and refreshes the calendar
aspects.extraDescription: Test: After edits, close the modal each way. Calendar cell summaries update on close.

aspects.description: Modal Save and Cancel buttons apply or discard edits
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Make edits in the modal, click Cancel — changes are discarded. Make edits again, click Save — changes are kept after modal close and page reload.

aspects.description: Weekly Kanban board shows Mon–Sun with previous and next week navigation
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Kanban view shows one Monday–Sunday week and previous/next week buttons change the displayed week.

aspects.description: Kanban selects the current week or week containing today on startup
aspects.extraDescription: Test: On first load, the board shows the current week or the week that contains today's date.

aspects.description: Only task and evaluation events appear on the Kanban board
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Schedule an other-type event in the current week. It does not appear on the board; task and evaluation events do.

aspects.description: Drag-and-drop between columns updates kanbanStatus and persists
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Drag a card between ToDo, InProgress, and Done. Status persists after page reload.

aspects.description: Kanban cards show type chip, displayName, name, date, and duration
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Each card has TASK (GIFTS blue) or EVALUATION (GIFTS green) chip, bold displayName, collection name, and details line e.g. 2026-06-17 · 3h.

aspects.description: Statistics show completed training days and planned vs completed hours
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Statistics view displays the number of completed training days (days where relevant task/evaluation work is marked done on the Kanban board) and total planned hours vs completed hours from persisted state.

aspects.description: Statistics list only collection entries with incomplete task or evaluation scheduling
aspects.extraDescription: Test: List shows only collection.json entries with fewer than 2 task assignments or fewer than 2 evaluation assignments, with current counts. Fully scheduled entries are omitted.

aspects.description: Day-coverage warnings when fewer than 120 or more than 122 days have events
aspects.extraDescription: Test: With fewer than 120 days with events, warning about n/120 is visible. If days exceed 122, maximum-exceeded warning is shown while the condition holds.
